title: The Mysterious Roswell Incident
story: Strange debris scattered across a ranch near Roswell, New Mexico, sparked a UFO frenzy in 1947. On July 7, the local newspaper reported a 'flying saucer' had been captured by the military. title: The Best Thing Since... Sliced Bread!
story: Bread lovers, rejoice! In 1928, a revolutionary invention changed breakfast tables forever. On July 7, the Chillicothe Baking Company in Missouri unveiled the first-ever machine-sliced bread. No more wonky knife work - perfectly even slices were now just a purchase away!

story: Whoosh! The space shuttle Atlantis zoomed back to Earth on July 7, 1995. After an exciting mission in orbit, the brave astronauts aboard STS-71 touched down safely. This flight marked a special moment in space history as Atlantis completed its 14th journey to the stars and back.

title: Columbia's Cosmic Journey Comes Home
story: Whoosh! The Space Shuttle Columbia streaked across the sky, returning from its stellar adventure. On July 7, 1996, after soaring through space, the shuttle touched down safely. This mission, known as STS-78, was Columbia's 20th voyage, packed with exciting experiments and discoveries. Astronauts worked hard, floating in zero gravity, to uncover new space secrets.

title: Basketball Teams Play Musical Chairs
story: Imagine basketball teams swapping cities like trading cards! That's what happened in 1978 when the NBA approved a wild franchise swap on July 7. The owners of the Buffalo Braves and Boston Celtics traded entire teams. The Braves' owner got the Celtics, while the Celtics' owner took control of the Braves. But the story didn't end there. The Braves soon packed their bags and moved to sunny San Diego, becoming the Clippers we know today.
activity_question: What team did the Buffalo Braves become after moving to a new city?
follow_up_question: Can you name another NBA team that has moved cities?

title: Wicket-Keeper's Incredible Cricket Feat
story: Stumps flew and catches were snatched as Ted Pooley showed off his lightning-fast reflexes. On July 7, 1868, this Surrey cricket team wicket-keeper amazed spectators at The Oval. During a match against Sussex, Pooley completed a jaw-dropping 12 dismissals - 8 catches and 4 stumpings. His nimble work behind the wicket set a new record in first-class cricket at the time.
activity_question: What was Ted Pooley's position on the cricket team?
follow_up_question: Can you explain what a 'stumping' is in cricket?
